# Publishing to Maven Central

Perfmon4j publishes artifacts to [Maven Central](https://central.sonatype.com/search?q=perfmon4j)
via the [Sonatype Central Portal](https://central.sonatype.com) using the
`central-publishing-maven-plugin`.

---

## How Publishing Is Triggered

Publishing runs automatically as part of the **Branch Push or Pull Request** GitHub Actions
workflow (`.github/workflows/maven.yml`). A deployment to Maven Central occurs when **all** of
the following are true:

- The event is a `push` (not a pull request)
- The commit message does **not** contain `[skip deploy]` or `#skip deploy#`
- At least one of:
  - The branch is `develop`, `master`, or matches `hotfix/*`
  - The commit message contains `[deploy]`, `[publish]`, or `#force deploy#`

Publishing can also be triggered manually at any time via the **Manual Workflow**
(`.github/workflows/manual.yml`) using the GitHub Actions "Run workflow" button.

---

## Snapshot vs. Release Artifacts

| Version type | Destination | Auto-published? |
|---|---|---|
| `x.y.z-SNAPSHOT` | `https://central.sonatype.com/repository/maven-snapshots/` | Yes (immediately) |
| `x.y.z` (release) | Maven Central via Sonatype Central Portal | Yes (`autoPublish=true`) |

The plugin detects the version type automatically. No workflow changes are needed between
snapshot and release builds.

> **Note for SNAPSHOT consumers:** Add the Sonatype Central snapshot repository to your
> project to resolve snapshot dependencies:
> ```xml
> <repository>
>     <id>central-snapshots</id>
>     <url>https://central.sonatype.com/repository/maven-snapshots/</url>
>     <snapshots><enabled>true</enabled></snapshots>
> </repository>
> ```

---

## Required GitHub Secrets

Navigate to **GitHub repo → Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions** and configure the
following secrets:

### `CENTRAL_USERNAME`
**What it is:** The token username generated by the Sonatype Central Portal.

**How to generate:**
1. Log in to [central.sonatype.com](https://central.sonatype.com)
2. Click your username (top right) → **View Account**
3. Scroll to **Generate User Token**
4. Click **Generate User Token** — copy the **username** value

### `CENTRAL_PASSWORD`
**What it is:** The token password generated alongside `CENTRAL_USERNAME`.

**How to generate:** Same steps as above — copy the **password** value from the same token.

> Tokens are shown only once. Store them securely before closing the dialog.

---

### `MAVEN_GPG_PRIVATE_KEY`
**What it is:** The ASCII-armored GPG private key used to sign all published artifacts.
Maven Central requires all artifacts to be signed.

**How to generate (if you don't have a key):**
```bash
gpg --gen-key
# Follow the prompts; use a strong passphrase
```

**How to export the private key:**
```bash
# List keys to find your key ID
gpg --list-secret-keys --keyid-format=long

# Export the private key (replace KEY_ID with your key's ID)
gpg --armor --export-secret-keys KEY_ID
```

Copy the entire output (including `-----BEGIN PGP PRIVATE KEY BLOCK-----` header/footer)
as the secret value.

**Also publish your public key** so Maven Central can verify signatures:
```bash
gpg --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --send-keys KEY_ID
```

### `MAVEN_GPG_PASSPHRASE`
**What it is:** The passphrase protecting the GPG private key above.

---

### `PACKAGES_GITHUB_USERNAME` and `PACKAGES_GITHUB_TOKEN`
These secrets are referenced in workflow comments for future GitHub Packages publishing
but are not currently used by any active workflow step. They can be added when GitHub
Packages deployment is implemented.

---

## Maven Command Used for Publishing

```bash
mvn -B deploy -DskipTests --file pom.xml \
  -P release,mvnCentral \
  -Dgpg.passphrase=${MAVEN_GPG_PASSPHRASE} \
  -Dcentral.userName=${CENTRAL_USERNAME} \
  -Dcentral.password=${CENTRAL_PASSWORD}
```

The `-P release,mvnCentral` activates two profiles:

| Profile | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `release` | Attaches Javadoc JARs, source JARs, and GPG signatures to all artifacts |
| `mvnCentral` | Activates the `central-publishing-maven-plugin` which bundles and uploads artifacts to the Central Portal |

---

## Relevant Files

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `pom.xml` | Defines the `release` and `mvnCentral` profiles |
| `.github/maven/settings.xml` | Maven server credentials template (copied to `~/.m2/settings.xml` by CI) |
| `.github/workflows/maven.yml` | Automatic publishing workflow |
| `.github/workflows/manual.yml` | Manual publishing workflow |
